LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ANNA KUZMINER'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF ANNA KUZMINER'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS SUPPORTED BY CALIFORNIA'S STRONG POLICY OF LIBERALITY IN AMENDMENT TO PLEADING. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT PLAINTIFF UNDULY DELAYED IN SEEKING LEAVE TO AMEND OR THAT IT WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE AMENDMENT IS ALLOWED. AS THE AMENDMENT WILL NOT AFFECT TWO OF THE THREE ORIGINAL CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT, AND PLAINTIFF IS NO LONGER PLEADING A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF A WRITTEN CONTRACT, DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION MAY BE HEARD AS SCHEDULED ON JUNE 7, 2019. DEFENDANT'S CHALLENGES TO THE NEW PROPOSED CAUSES OF ACTION AS "FUTILE" MAY BE RAISED BY DEMURRER OR A FURTHER MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION/SUMMARY JUDGMENT. IF DEFENDANT SEEKS TO PRESERVE THE EXISTING TRIAL DATE, IT MAY APPLY EX PARTE FOR LEAVE TO FILE SUCH A FURTHER MOTION ON SHORTENED TIME. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-06-2020 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-15-2019 Continued To Mar-23-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-15-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel Ray Bacon, Esq., Law Offices of Daniel Ray Bacon, Telephone 415-864-0907, appearing for Plaintiff;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANNA KUZMINER's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff Anna Kuzminer's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. The motion is supported by California's strong policy of liberality in amendment to pleading. Defendant has not shown that Plaintiff unduly delayed in seeking leave to amend or that it will be prejudiced if the amendment is allowed. As the amendment will not affect two of the three original causes of action in the complaint, and Plaintiff is no longer pleading a cause of action for breach of a written contract, Defendant's motion for summary judgment or summary adjudication as to those causes of action may be heard as scheduled on June 7, 2019. Defendant's challenges to the new proposed causes of action as "futile" may be raised by demurrer or a further motion for summary adjudication/summary judgment. If Defendant seeks to preserve the existing trial date, it may apply ex parte for leave to file such a further motion on shortened time.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-21-2018 Set For Trial On Jul-08-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
